section.title: Beyond Work
section.subtitle: What I get up to away from the keyboard — and a few things I build just for fun.

topics.title: Adventures & Sports
topics.summary: Trails, peaks, volleyball, and a good game of football.
topics.body_html: I love the outdoors, and hiking is my thing 🏔️. I've climbed some of the highest peaks in Brazil and once completed a 250 km long-distance trek. My next big dream — one I plan to make happen soon — is to walk the Camino de Santiago. When I'm not on a trail, you'll find me playing volleyball 🏐 or, like any good Brazilian, soccer ⚽.

topics.title: Open Source
topics.summary: Building on each other's solutions so we all get better technology.
topics.body_html:
I love the idea behind open source: one person's solution can become a stepping stone for someone else's problem — and that person can give back in the same way. In the end, we all benefit from more mature, more capable technology.

Right now I'm contributing to a Django + HTMX component library, where I've been collecting the patterns I reach for every day while building real projects.

topics.title: Tools & Pastimes I Build
topics.summary: Taking small everyday annoyances and coding my own fix.
topics.body_html:
I love taking a small everyday problem and building my own solution for it. Here are a couple of them:

  • Quem? — a free, ad-free web version of the "Who am I?" party game I play with friends. The Play Store apps are buried in ads and paywalls, so I built my own.
  • PTAX Converter — in Brazil, invoicing foreign clients means converting the amounts using a special Central Bank rate. The official site is painful to use, so I took their API and wrapped it in a nicer interface.
